description: Milky white pork bone broth ramen from Fukuoka's legendary yatai street stalls.
teaser: The broth is white because of what happens when you boil pork bones hard enough, long enough. Most people have never tasted it this way.

steps.slap_context: making tonkotsu broth for hakata ramen
slicing chashu pork for hakata ramen

tripo_prompt: Hakata tonkotsu ramen bowl with white creamy broth, chashu pork, soft boiled egg, green onions, noodles, Japanese food, top-down view, white background
seedance_prompt: Japanese yatai street stall chef preparing hakata tonkotsu ramen, steam rising from milky white broth, night street scene, cinematic food documentary style
guide_personality: boisterous, passionate about Fukuoka street food, friendly and loud
